Our younger cat who has not lived with dogs is much more wary but is getting used to him and he is always great with either cat.


Pogo is wonderful with my neighbors small kids and loves hanging out with my older kids. He loves to snuggle up, either at your feet or next to you on the couch. He learns very quickly, both tricks and the rules and expectations.


He is not a big fetching dog, but will do it a few times to make you happy. He does play with his toys and chews on bones. He loves playing in whatever way is offered.


He definitely prefers to be near humans he views as safe. He seems friendly to other dogs if introduced gently. He would not be happy left alone for long periods, at least until he was much more certain of his people. He is absolutely zero aggression, even when nervous. He does have slight separation anxiety but it is improving over time.


He does appear to have a chicken allergy and likely also reacts to dairy. We are testing to confirm the food issues. He gets super itchy and red quickly, but it also disappears quickly when the food and treats are fixed.

name: Roux
description_html: Roux was on his own for quite some time. He was staying on an abandoned property in east Austin. Neighbors slowly made friends and Roux went directly into foster with a neighbor. He is shy at first, but opens up to people quickly. He loves people, treats, and affection. Roux is a laid back dog that loves to hang out in the yard, snuggle, or play with his people. He is house trained and is learning all the tricks. He’s a very smart boy!

name: Kianu
description_html: Kianu is a very unhappy camper here! He was brought to the shelter when his owners lost their home. Now, he’s stuck in a kennel and hating life. He’s lived with cats before so may do well in a home with feline friends after a slow acclimation. In the home, Kianu was loving, cuddly, playful and eager to listen and hang out with his people. In the shelter, he’s stressed out and will smack us around. We'd love to get him into foster care ASAP so he can show off his affectionate side!

name: Buckwheat
description_html:
Meet Buckwheat 🤎

Buckwheat is a gentle soul who’s still finding his footing in the busy shelter environment. All the sights and sounds can be a bit overwhelming for him—but once he gets a chance to relax, his sweet and silly personality really starts to shine.

Out in a play yard, Buckwheat is an absolute delight. He loves to stretch his legs with little joyful runs during his breaks, showing off a playful side that’s sure to make you smile. Despite the stress of shelter life, he’s been impressively well-mannered and appears to be very housetrained.

Buckwheat is a quiet, laid-back boy who would much prefer a calm, peaceful setting where he can decompress at his own pace. On leash, he can be a bit nervous at first, but you’ll notice him relax more and more as you move away from the shelter environment.

What stands out most about Buckwheat is his heart—you can tell he truly wants to connect and accept love, even while he’s still building confidence. The best way to win him over is simple: patience, kindness, and letting him come to you when he’s ready.

In the right home, Buckwheat is going to blossom into a loyal, loving companion who will make every bit of that patience worth it.

name: Fern
description_html: Pretty Fern is a dream! She is super easy to leash and go and is a great companion on walks. This sweet girl is affectionate, well behaved and ready for her forever home.

name: Hobbes
description_html: Hobbes is perfect with a capital P. He doesn’t react to dogs, birds, or squirrels on walks. He walks alongside you with a loose leash. He LOVES cuddles and pets more than anything else. Hobbes would probably live happily in an apartment; he's quiet and small-sized, and really just wants to snuggle with people. Hobbes is a social butterfly waiting for his new friends to invite him to happy hour!

name: Darrell
description_html: Darrell is the sweetest snugglebug around! He is very gentle and loves to be next to his people. He is good natured, very tolerant, and friendly toward other dogs and people. He gets nervous sometimes in new spaces, but we are working on building his confidence! He does well on car rides and on his leash, and he’s not a barker. Peanut butter is his favorite treat, and he is super cute when he dreams! Everywhere we go, people stop to say hi and love on him! He’d make a great addition to a family or a person who wants a dog that’s social and will develop a deep bond with you.

name: Leapus Maximus
description_html: Leapus Maximus came in with several other cats from an outdoor area where they were being temporarily cared for. He started out shy, but has become a true cuddle bug! He will eagerly accept and appreciate pets. We also think he'd love to play if given the space to do so (instead of a kennel)! Leapus may prefer to be your only cat or requires very slow introductions in a home. He loves people and loves attention!

name: Gypsy
description_html:
A family lifestyle with dogs, cats and 2 children suited Gypsy beautifully.

As a cultured lady she knows her manners, her sit, stay, and she walks nicely on a leash and is house trained.

Her favorite things? Cuddles, walks, play and all things affectionate and loving

Gypsy is vaccinated, microchipped, heart worm negative, and spayed and although large, she's a shorter stature and has room to drop a few pounds

See her short video of when she was getting familiar with the shelter.

Gypsy is going to make a lucky family very happy.

She's advocated for by ClassicCaninesATX who provide an extra layer of tailored support for the mature dogs. Use her link to reach to reach out for questions or a meet up.

name: Banjo
description_html:
Meet Banjo! 🎶🐾


Banjo is an estimated 2-year-old pup with a curious nose and a gentle, observant nature. On walks, he’s in his element—taking his time to sniff and explore the world around him. Every scent is a story, and Banjo is eager to read them all!


He’s also shown interest in other dogs, often watching them with a calm curiosity that suggests he may enjoy canine companionship with the right introductions. With a little time and guidance, Banjo could make a wonderful walking buddy or even a great addition to a multi-dog home.


Right now, Banjo is staying in a crate in a busy hallway due to the shelter being overcapacity. It’s not an ideal spot for a dog who clearly enjoys getting out and experiencing the world, and we’d love to find him a foster or forever home where he can stretch his legs, relax, and truly shine.


If you’re looking for a sweet, curious companion who will happily join you on slow, sniff-filled strolls, Banjo might just be your perfect match. Come meet him and help him trade hallway noise for a cozy home! 💛

name: Seven
description_html: Looking for a beauty with tiger stripes? Here she is! Seven is an absolute doll and a sweetheart. She’s a very gentle girl who is thrilled to get out for her exercise walks. Seven strolls along easily on leash and sits like a princess for treats. She's all about companionship and happiest when she has the company of her friends. You'll never be lonely with this girl around!

name: Inspector Tater Tot
description_html: Inspector Tater Tot was living at a RV park and camp site for the past several years. He is definitely used to his freedom and may enjoy an indoor/outdoor or catio lifestyle. He's curious, calm and enjoys pets in moderation. When he's not solving crimes, like the infamous case of the missing Churu, he enjoys lounging, eating snacks, birding, and nature walks. Inspector has completed his latest investigation into campsite living, and is now ready to explore home options!

name: Kevin
description_html: Kevin was surrendered due to not doing well in a home with other cats. He loves people and would prefer not to share the attention! He's a cuddly guy who enjoys pets and snacks (as you can see), and will follow you around to make sure you don't get lonely. Kevin sometimes nips if he gets overstimulated, which can be easily managed in a home. Most of the time, he just wants to lounge around and be the center of attention. This big boy will be a loving, loyal companion to the lucky family who adopts him!

name: Olive
description_html: Olive is a shy girl who is very gentle and loving with people, cozying up to all the new friends she's made at the shelter so far. She has been easy to walk on leash and regularly checks in for direction and will stop for pets and comfort. She is looking for a home without cats or chickens and farm animals. She may do well in a home with dogs and slow introductions. Olive is ready to brighten your day!

name: Cinnamon
description_html: Cinnamon was abandoned at a rescue and then brought to us for care. She's a shy girl, which we totally understand given the ups and downs of her journey to us. Cinnamon loves Churu, so that may be the best way to make introductions. She enjoys pets and once she's in a calm home setting, we think that's when she will truly blossom! So add some spice to your life and adopt Cinnamon today!

name: Zuli
description_html: Zuli is shy at first but warms up and loooooooves people. She is so sweet. She loves snuggles, head and butt scratches, and belly rubs. She likes to play but is a chill energy for just a year old. She is great with other dogs, small and big kids.

name: Olaf
description_html: Olaf will steal your heart if you spend even two minutes with him! He's playful and loves to romp around like a big, goofy, good natured boy. He is cuddly and calm even if his neighboring dog friends are barking. Olaf waits patiently to be walked each day and does his best to show off his good manners on leash. He also appreciates the opportunity to zoom around the yard and stretch his legs. Olaf would prefer to be lounging on your sofa than stuck in a kennel!

Hi there! I’m Pixie, a sweet little girl with a heart of gold. I’m a young pup, full of energy and love to brighten your day with my playful spirit. I’m super friendly and always eager to meet new people, so if you're looking for a cuddle buddy or a lively playmate, I’m your girl!


I have a charming bark and enjoy expressing myself—it’s just part of my personality! After a fun day of play, there’s nothing I love more than curling up next to you for some quality snuggle time. I’ve been great about my potty training and have been going outside to do my business.


I get along wonderfully with my other small dogs in my foster home. I haven’t been seen interacting with large dogs or many other pups so I might still need to see how I do with them.


If you’re ready to welcome a loving companion who will bring joy and laughter into your life, I can’t wait to meet you!

name: Hartley
description_html:
Gentle Hartley! This special, shy boy just wants a friend to talk to. He has the sweetest meow and will talk up a storm once he feels comfortable with you.


Hartley lived outdoors as a community cat until he was brought to the shelter through the SNR program. Once he got here, he had some time to decompress which is where he began to shine! We knew then that he was a social boy who deserves a safe, indoor home. He's beginning to feel comfortable enough to ask for pets and will thread himself between your legs to let you know when he's ready for affection.

name: Billy
description_html:
Meet Billy! 🐾


Billy is a 2‑month‑old bundle of sweetness who’s already come so far. This adorable pup had a rough start — he was found covered in fleas and ticks, his little tummy full of hookworms, and heartbreakingly, he’d been thrown from a truck. But Billy didn’t let that stop him.


Today, Billy is healthy, happy, and thriving! He’s eating like a champ, mastering his potty training, and showing off his playful, curious spirit every day. He adores both dogs and cats, loves to nap (a lot!), rides wonderfully in the car, and even sleeps soundly through the night.


Billy is proof that resilience comes in small, furry packages — and he’s ready to find a family who’ll give him the love he’s always deserved.


If you’d like to know more or meet Billy, just click the “Get Started” button!

name: Sassy Girl
description_html:
Sassy Girl entered the shelter system when her owner could no longer care for her. We've found her to be a very friendly lady while she's housed in a crate waiting for life's next adventure.

She's very house trained and quiet in her crate.

In the play yard she likes to treat the swimming pool as her giant water bowl, drinking fresh water but never dipping her toes in.

She's easy to walk and easy to love and we encourage you to meet her and fall in love.

She's spayed and heart worm negative and ready to leave right away for a loving and safe indoors opportunity.

name: Pickles
description_html: Pickles has been sweet and playful. She plays with kids and has been patient with them and sleeps a lot.

name: Clove
description_html: Clove's hobbies are chasing lizards, keeping up with what the squirrels are doing, and exploring his world one sniff at a time. He is still working on his leash skills during those times when his hobbies distract him. Clove enjoys a cuddle once he gets to know you, so we encourage you to get to know this unique boy!
